questions.question: Prove that the sum of angles of a triangle is 180°.
questions.answer: Draw a line parallel to one side through the opposite vertex; alternate interior angles and linear pair show sum of interior angles = 180°.

questions.question: Prove the Pythagoras theorem using triangle similarity.
questions.answer: Consider right-angled triangle, draw altitude from right angle to hypotenuse; use similarity of triangles to show hypotenuse² = sum of squares of other two sides.

questions.question: Prove that the exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of two opposite interior angles.
questions.answer: Extend one side; using linear pair and angle sum property, exterior angle = sum of opposite interior angles.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side and half of it.
questions.answer: Use triangle properties and coordinate method or vector method to show midpoint line is parallel and half the third side.

questions.question: Prove that the medians of a triangle intersect at a point (centroid).
questions.answer: Draw medians, use coordinate method to show intersection point divides each median in 2:1 ratio.

questions.question: Prove that the angle bisectors of a triangle intersect at a point (incentre).
questions.answer: Use angle bisector properties and perpendicular distances from sides to show concurrent point is equidistant from all sides.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular bisectors of a triangle intersect at a point (circumcentre).
questions.answer: Use perpendicular bisector properties to show concurrency at a point equidistant from all vertices.

questions.question: Prove that the altitudes of a triangle intersect at a point (orthocentre).
questions.answer: Draw altitudes; use similar triangles or coordinate geometry to show altitudes are concurrent at a point.

questions.question: Prove that in similar triangles, corresponding sides are proportional.
questions.answer: Use AA criterion for similarity; compare ratios of corresponding sides using similarity of triangles.

questions.question: Prove that in similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal.
questions.answer: By AA criterion or SAS similarity, show corresponding angles of similar triangles are equal.

questions.question: Use the Pythagoras theorem to find the length of the hypotenuse given two sides.
questions.answer: Apply hypotenuse² = sum of squares of other two sides and solve for hypotenuse.

questions.question: Use the Pythagoras theorem to find a missing side of a right-angled triangle.
questions.answer: Subtract square of known side from hypotenuse² or use other side² = hypotenuse² - known side².

questions.question: Prove that the medians of a triangle divide it into six smaller triangles of equal area.
questions.answer: Show that medians intersect at centroid, each median divides triangle into equal areas; total 6 triangles of equal area.

questions.question: Prove that in a right-angled triangle, the median to the hypotenuse equals half of the hypotenuse.
questions.answer: Draw median; use coordinate geometry or properties of right triangle to show median = hypotenuse/2.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.
questions.answer: Use triangle inequality property to show sum of any two sides > third side.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than twice the length of the median to the third side.
questions.answer: Use median formula and triangle inequality to establish the result.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the centroid and a vertex divides the median in a 2:1 ratio.
questions.answer: Use centroid definition; divide median into two parts, larger segment towards vertex = 2/3 of median.

questions.question: Prove that the angle bisector divides the opposite side in the ratio of adjacent sides.
questions.answer: Use triangle properties to show angle bisector divides opposite side in proportion of other two sides.

questions.question: Prove that the centroid, circumcentre, incentre, and orthocentre coincide in an equilateral triangle.
questions.answer: Use symmetry and triangle properties to show all four centers coincide.

questions.question: Use similarity of triangles to find the height of a building using its shadow.
questions.answer: Form right-angled triangles of building and stick, set up proportion of corresponding sides, solve for height.

questions.question: Use similarity of triangles to find the distance between two points using shadows.
questions.answer: Form similar triangles, equate corresponding sides, solve for distance.

questions.question: Use the mid-point theorem to prove that a quadrilateral formed by joining midpoints of triangle sides is a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Join midpoints; apply midpoint theorem to show opposite sides parallel; hence, quadrilateral is parallelogram.

questions.question: Use Pythagoras theorem to check whether a triangle is right-angled.
questions.answer: Check if square of longest side = sum of squares of other two sides.

questions.question: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite equal angles are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence criteria (ASA or AAS) to show sides opposite equal angles are equal.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a triangle = 1/2 × base × height.
questions.answer: Consider triangle with base and height; area formula follows from rectangle area by halving.
